meridian_skill.description: Universal migration from Obsidian, Notion, Logseq, markdown, CSV, JSON, Roam
meridian_skill.name: migrate

sections.heading: Contract
lines: checklist:ai-autonomy:

sections.heading: Supported Sources
lines: use judgment to follow the Supported Sources guidance:

table:decision:

sections.heading: Phases
lines: use judgment to migrate an external source into the brain:
Assess the source format, file count, and structure
Plan how source fields map to gbrain fields (type, title, tags, compiled truth, timeline)
Test with a sample of five to ten files and verify by reading them back
Bulk import the full directory, then verify health and spot-check pages
Extract cross-references and create typed links

sections.heading: Obsidian Migration
lines: use judgment to follow the Obsidian Migration guidance:
Import the vault directory into gbrain (Obsidian vaults are markdown directories)
Wire the graph with native wikilink support (v0.12.1+):
codeblock:bash:Z2JyYWluIGV4dHJhY3QgbGlua3MgLS1zb3VyY2UgZGIgLS1kcnktcnVuIHwgaGVhZCAtMjAgICAgIyBwcmV2aWV3CmdicmFpbiBleHRyYWN0IGxpbmtzIC0tc291cmNlIGRiICAgICAgICAgICAgICAgICAgICAgICAgICMgY29tbWl0
`extract links` natively parses `[[relative/path]]` and `[[relative/path|Display Text]]`
alongside standard `[text](page.md)` markdown syntax. Ancestor-search resolution handles
wiki KBs where authors omit one or more leading `../` prefixes. The `.md` suffix is
inferred automatically for wikilinks
Obsidian-specific:
item: Tags (`#tag`) become gbrain tags
item: Frontmatter properties map to gbrain frontmatter
item: Attachments (images, PDFs) are noted but handled separately via file storage

sections.heading: Notion Migration
lines: Export from Notion: Settings > Export > Markdown & CSV
Notion exports nested directories with UUIDs in filenames
Strip UUIDs from filenames for clean slugs
Map Notion's database properties to frontmatter
Import the cleaned directory into gbrain

sections.heading: CSV Migration
lines: For tabular data (e.g., CRM exports, contact lists):
For each row in the CSV, create a page with column values as frontmatter
Use a designated column as the slug (e.g., name)
Use another column as compiled_truth (e.g., notes)
Store each page in gbrain

sections.heading: Verification
lines: After any migration:
Check gbrain statistics to verify page count matches source
Check gbrain health for orphans and missing embeddings
Export pages from gbrain for round-trip verification
Spot-check 5-10 pages by reading them from gbrain
Test search: search gbrain for "someone you know is in the data"

sections.heading: Tools Used
lines: Store/update pages in gbrain (put_page)
Read pages from gbrain (get_page)
Link entities in gbrain (add_link)
Tag pages in gbrain (add_tag)
Get gbrain statistics (get_stats)
Check gbrain health (get_health)
Search gbrain (query)
